checks.a_analysis: The conversion faithfully follows the content of the source PDF. The metadata title 'Near Perfect Powerpoint Slides By Accessibility Experts 47B0' is AI-generated for accessibility purposes, which is an acceptable change according to instructions. The content within the sections corresponds to the text and elements found in the source slides without hallucinations or invented body text.

The phone number '833-294-2257' in the output matches the source document. There are no invented or mismatched email addresses, URLs, or physical addresses in the output. The omission of specific contact details is addressed in check C5.

The source document contains 60 pages, and the metadata correctly reflects a page count of 60. The omission markers in the provided JSON indicate that sections were removed for token budget constraints during display, rather than being missed during the conversion process itself. The beginning and end of the document are present.

The tables on page 8 and page 23 were examined. The table representing form schedules on page 8 has 8 data rows in both the source and the output. The color contrast matrix on page 23 has 6 data rows in both the source and output. No material omission of rows was detected.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: In the 'Get in touch' section on page 60, significant contact information is missing. The source lists four primary contact details: Company name, Website, Phone, and Email. The output omits the website ('Web: www.ablr360.com') and the email ('Email: kim.casey@ablr360.com'). Additionally, the instruction text 'Scan QR code to Ablr contact page' is missing. This constitutes an omission of approximately 40% of the textual information within this specific contact section, exceeding the 25% threshold for a Major defect.
checks.b_evidence_source:
Web: www.ablr360.com
Email: kim.casey@ablr360.com
Scan QR code to Ablr contact page
checks.b_evidence_output: The text is entirely absent from the corresponding section in the output.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: In the 'Accessible color combinations' table on page 23, there are transcription errors in the pass/fail indicators ('Aa'). Specifically, in the row for 'Black background #000000', the output incorrectly leaves the cell for 'Light blue text #348AD7' empty (fail) when the source shows it as 'Aa' (pass). Conversely, it incorrectly places 'Aa' in the cell for 'Navy text #092940' when the source shows it as empty. This provides incorrect accessibility guidance compared to the source material.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table on page 23, row 'Black background #000000': Light blue text column contains 'Aa'; Navy text column is empty.
checks.b_evidence_output: Table at reading_order 150, row 1: Cell 4 (Light blue) is empty; Cell 6 (Navy) contains 'Aa'.

checks.a_analysis: After a thorough review of the provided output against the source document, no instances of duplicated text blocks or sections were identified. Each element in the JSON appears to correspond to a unique entry in the original PDF.

The heading hierarchy is consistent throughout the document. Slide titles are correctly identified as level 1 headings (H1), and sub-sections within individual slides (such as on pages 8, 23, and 26) are correctly nested as level 2 headings (H2). There are no instances of skipped heading levels.

The tables identified in the output (Pages 8, 23, 26, and 57) accurately represent the complex visual data found in the source images. For example, on Page 8, the conversion successfully linearizes a series of form fields into a logical row-and-column structure. On Page 23, the color contrast grid is faithfully reproduced. The mapping of headers and data cells is structurally sound.

On page 60, the reading order is logically flawed and incomplete. The heading 'Thank You!', which acts as a concluding remark in the source, is placed between the 'Get in touch' heading and the actual contact details. This disrupts the intended flow where the 'Get in touch' section should immediately present the contact information. Furthermore, three distinct blocks of text ('Web: www.ablr360.com', 'Email: kim.casey@ablr360.com', and 'Scan QR code to Ablr contact page') are omitted from the structural sequence entirely.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 60 layout shows 'Get in touch' followed by contact info, with 'Thank You!' as a separate concluding element on the right.

checks.a_analysis: The lists provided in the JSON (e.g., the Agenda on Page 3 and the numbered/bulleted lists on pages 8, 23, and 57) match the structure and nesting of the source document.

On page 60, critical contact links are missing. While the phone number is preserved, the website (www.ablr360.com) and email address (kim.casey@ablr360.com) are absent from the output, failing to provide the actionable items present in the source.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text blocks and associated links for the website and email are missing from the elements list for Page 60.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output successfully converts the PowerPoint slide deck into a structured, single-column web document. The visual organization follows the original slide sequence, making it easy to find information in its original context. Meaningful layout choices, such as grouping related text and images, have been preserved in a responsive HTML format.

The tables present on slides 29, 30, 31, and the summary table generated for slide 57 are all rendered correctly as HTML tables. They feature clear borders, distinct header rows, and well-aligned cell content, making the data easily readable and understandable by a sighted user.

All meaningful images from the source PDF, including photographs (e.g., slides 2, 7, 53), icons (e.g., slides 5, 6, 12, 47), diagrams (e.g., slides 14, 24, 36), and software screenshots (e.g., slides 19, 25, 35, 57), are correctly present and rendered in the HTML output. No broken image links or missing content images were found.

There is no evidence of duplicated body content such as paragraphs or tables. While document titles and slide headings are repeated in the navigation menu/table of contents, this is an intentional and expected accessibility feature of the HTML conversion.
